categories.name: CSS Fundamentals
categories.description: Box model, specificity, flexbox, and grid, learned by doing

categories.name: General Dev Quests
categories.description: Cross-cutting developer challenges and CLI-focused drills

categories.name: Git & Version Control
categories.description: Branching, rebasing, and Git workflows explained interactively

categories.name: JavaScript Fundamentals
categories.description: Closures, async, and core JS concepts through interactive challenges

quests.name: Abyssal Current
quests.shortDescription: A bioluminescent deep-sea simulator that teaches CSS @keyframes, animation timing, and easing functions through environmental survival.
quests.longDescription:
## Abyssal Current

Pilot a glowing submersible through pitch-black ocean trenches where the only light comes from CSS animations you construct. Drag keyframe "buoys" onto a timeline reel to build `@keyframes` while your sub's bioluminescence animates live.

### Features

- Real-time CSS injection engine updates the game world instantly as you manipulate the timeline.
- Physical drag-and-drop keyframe buoys representing specific CSS transformations, scale, and hue rotations.
- Live easing visualizer that demonstrates the mathematical difference between linear and ease-in-out functions using particle currents.
- Ghost UI hints and survival mechanics: An oxygen/pressure gauge that steadily drains, forcing players to quickly grasp and apply the correct animation sequence to clear the sector.
- High-fidelity, procedurally animated SVG deep-sea creatures (Jellyfish, Anglerfish, Octopus, Seahorse) that sync with your animation states.
- Fully responsive layout utilizing raw Vanilla JS and CSS variables, with absolutely zero external frameworks.

quests.name: Blame Court
quests.shortDescription: A courtroom drama simulator where you play a defense attorney cross-examining git commit histories to find the true source of a bug.
quests.longDescription:
## Blame Court

Step into the courtroom and defend your code! In this trial simulator, you play a defense attorney tasked with proving which commit actually introduced a bug. You must look past recent, superficial changes and cross-examine the real commit history to find the true culprit.

### Features

- **Witness Stand Diffs:** Review actual code diffs on the witness stand to see exactly what changed and when.
- **Timeline Recall:** Trace a buggy line back through history, mimicking the real-world `git blame` and `git log -p` workflow.
- **Red Herring Prosecutor:** Battle an AI prosecutor who presents misleading commits with deceptive messages or superficial formatting tweaks.
- **Evidence Board:** Build a visual timeline of a line's history as you piece together the case.
- **The Verdict:** Learn the vital difference between who last touched a line and who is actually "guilty" of a bug's root cause.
- **Zero-Dependency Engine:** Flawless responsive layout and dramatic courtroom animations built entirely natively.

quests.name: Branch Navigator
quests.shortDescription: A lightweight Git quest (Easy) to learn checkout basics by navigating a commit graph with `git log`, `git branch`, and `git checkout`.
quests.longDescription:
## Branch Navigator

Navigate a commit graph like a time traveler. Start from the newest commit and use Git commands in an interactive terminal to reach specific commits and branches.

### Features

- 3 easy levels
- Learn `git log`, `git branch`, and `git checkout`
- Visual commit graph with HEAD and branch labels
- Goal-based terminal completion + progress saved locally
- Fully offline, single-file quest

quests.name: Closure Escape Room
quests.shortDescription: Escape puzzle rooms by solving JavaScript closure, lexical scope, callback, and private state challenges.
quests.longDescription:
## Closure Escape Room

An escape-room themed JavaScript quest where every locked door is opened by solving a closure puzzle. Learn how closures capture variables, understand lexical scope, debug common mistakes, and master private state through interactive challenges.

### Features

- 10 progressively challenging escape rooms
- Learn closures, lexical scope, callbacks, private variables, and data encapsulation
- Interactive puzzles with real-time feedback and explanations
- Hint system for difficult challenges
- Progress saved locally using localStorage
- Responsive and keyboard accessible
- Works completely offline

quests.name: Dead Scope
quests.shortDescription: A horror-themed JavaScript quiz — 10 hauntings covering closures, shadowing, hoisting, and loop bindings. Wrong answers drain your sanity and darken the room.
quests.longDescription:
## Dead Scope

A horror reskin of Closure Chronicles built on one premise: a closure is a variable that refuses to die when its function does. Ten functions have died in this machine — trace what still lingers.

### Features

- 10 hauntings covering global, function, block, and lexical scope — each retitled to match its concept (hoisting becomes "The Risen", var-in-loops becomes "Three Ghosts, One Grave")
- Reactive dread system: mistakes trigger a screen quake and blood-red flash, and permanently deepen the ambient vignette
- Live sanity meter (−15 per mistake, −5 per hint)
- Graveyard progress tracker — one headstone per level, marked † when cleared
- "Listen to the Walls" hint system
- Badges: Unscathed, Deaf to the Whispers, Exorcist of Scope
- Candle rating (1–3) and falling-ash finale
- Analog-horror aesthetic: CRT scanlines, VT323 + IM Fell English, code framed as recovered exhibits
- Respects prefers-reduced-motion; progress saved via localStorage

quests.name: Event Loop Arena
quests.shortDescription: Predict JavaScript's event loop output order across 10 increasingly tricky levels.
quests.longDescription:
## Event Loop Arena

Master JavaScript's event loop by predicting the execution order of synchronous code, microtasks, macrotasks, Promises, async/await, and timers.

### Features

- 10 progressively harder levels
- Covers synchronous execution, setTimeout, Promise.then, async/await, and nested event loop behavior
- Real-time explanations after every level
- Progress saved locally via localStorage
- Responsive and keyboard accessible
- Works completely offline

quests.name: Flexbox Space Dock
quests.shortDescription: A futuristic space cargo simulator where you play as a Docking Officer directing heavy freight containers into shipping vessels using CSS Flexbox.
quests.longDescription:
## Flexbox Space Dock

A futuristic space cargo simulator where you play as a Docking Officer directing heavy freight containers into shipping vessels. Each spaceship cargo bay has custom target shapes and layouts. You must use CSS Flexbox properties to pack and align the containers perfectly before the launch countdown expires.

### Features

- **Live Space Cargo Bay:** An interactive visualizer showing cargo containers drifting or snapping into place in real time as Flexbox properties are adjusted.
- **Interactive Control Deck:** A premium sci-fi control panel where you select and toggle flex values, with visual representations of the main and cross axes.
- **Dynamic Loading Shipments:** Progressive levels with varying container dimensions, testing advanced concepts like flex-grow, flex-shrink, flex-basis, and order.
- **Emergency Departure Countdown:** A soft-pressure timer that adds dynamic visual effects (warning sirens, thruster steam) as time runs low to encourage quick thinking.
- **Local Storage Progress:** Saves unlocked ranks (e.g., from "Cargo Cadet" to "Chief Logistics Commander") and completed levels in localStorage.

quests.name: Git Bisect Detective
quests.shortDescription: A noir detective mystery that teaches git bisect and binary search debugging by having you interrogate suspects to find the guilty commit.
quests.longDescription:
## Git Bisect Detective

A bug slipped into the codebase somewhere in the commit history. Play as a detective interrogating suspect commits to narrow down exactly which one introduced the bug — a gamified version of the binary search algorithm git bisect uses under the hood.

### Features

- Full noir detective theme with an original low-poly animated hero illustration
- Genuine binary search gameplay — choose which suspect to interrogate each round, with the mathematically optimal choice flagged as the "Prime Suspect"
- Three case sizes (8 / 16 / 32 suspects) with in-world difficulty names
- A Case Briefing screen explaining both the in-fiction rules and the real git bisect concept side by side
- Detective Rating system (Rookie through Living Legend) based on efficiency versus optimal play
- Best rank per difficulty saved locally
- Fully responsive, keyboard accessible, respects reduced-motion preferences
- Works completely offline

quests.name: Git Pirate Adventure
quests.shortDescription: Embark on a pirate adventure to master Git by completing treasure-hunting missions across mysterious islands.
quests.longDescription:
## Git Pirate Adventure

Set sail across a chain of pirate islands where each island teaches a core Git concept. Recover the legendary treasure map by completing interactive challenges involving commits, branching, merging, rebasing, stashing, cherry-picking, and reflog recovery.

### Features

- 10 pirate islands with progressively harder Git challenges
- Interactive treasure-hunting gameplay
- Learn commits, branches, merge, rebase, stash, cherry-pick, and reflog
- Unlock new islands by completing previous missions
- Hint system with explanations after every challenge
- Pirate Rank and treasure rewards based on performance
- Progress saved using localStorage
- Responsive and keyboard accessible
- Works completely offline

quests.name: Merge Conflict: Frontline
quests.shortDescription: A tactical war/heist strategy game that teaches Git merge conflict resolution by defusing increasingly complex conflicts under pressure.
quests.longDescription:
## Merge Conflict: Frontline

Two branches. One codebase. Every merge conflict is a live wire.

Step into the role of a tactical field operative responsible for resolving critical Git merge conflicts before the operation collapses. Study competing changes from both branches, decide which lines to keep, discard, or combine, and work through increasingly difficult scenarios inspired by real-world merge conflicts.

### Features

- Immersive war/heist themed Git merge conflict strategy game
- Authentic Git conflict markers (`<<<<<<<`, `=======`, `>>>>>>>`) for real conflict resolution practice
- Five progressively difficult missions covering simple conflicts, multi-hunk merges, dependent conflicts, and full-file conflict resolution
- Tactical decision system where players choose to keep HEAD, keep incoming changes, or intelligently merge both versions
- Mission briefings, squad morale, operation timer, and mission progression create increasing tension
- Detailed debrief after every conflict explaining the correct merge strategy and underlying Git concept
- Final Field Manual summarizing merge conflict markers, HEAD vs incoming changes, merge strategies, and related Git terminology
- Military-inspired HUD with custom SVG graphics, immersive animations, responsive design, and local progress saving
- Fully self-contained single HTML file built with vanilla web technologies and no external JavaScript dependencies

quests.name: Patch Kitchen
quests.shortDescription: A short-order diner simulator that teaches JavaScript array methods by turning .map(), .filter(), and .reduce() into literal cooking actions.
quests.longDescription:
## Patch Kitchen

Step into a fast-paced short-order diner where JavaScript array methods are your cooking tools. Transform raw ingredients in your sizzling pan to match specific customer orders. By turning abstract data mutations into tangible culinary actions, you'll master how arrays work under the hood.

### Features

- **Literal Array Cooking:** `.push()` adds ingredients, `.filter()` picks out the burnt bits, `.map()` seasons everything at once, and `.reduce()` plates the final combined dish.
- **Tangible State Management:** Drag physical ingredient icons into a pan and watch the array visually mutate with every tool you tap.
- **Customer Order Tickets:** Match your pan's array to target outputs disguised as diner tickets (e.g., "Vegetarian only, seasoned").
- **Satisfying Feedback:** Enjoy a satisfying "ding!" and plating animation on a successful order, or watch your ingredients fly off the pan when you mess up the recipe.
- **Bite-Sized Progression:** Master the core array methods across 5-6 rapid-fire orders in under 10 minutes.
- **Zero-Dependency Engine:** Flawless responsive layout and smooth 60fps animations built entirely natively.

quests.name: Promise Pipeline: Steampunk Flow
quests.shortDescription: A Victorian steampunk puzzle game where asynchronous tasks are represented by pressurized steam flow. Use Promises to connect boilers to machinery.
quests.longDescription:
## Promise Pipeline: Steampunk Flow

Step into a Victorian steam laboratory where asynchronous JavaScript operations are powered by pressurized steam. Connect steam boilers (Promise producers) to heavy gears and engines (consumers) using copper pipes (`.then()`), release valves (`.catch()`), and cleanup vents (`.finally()`). Learn the mechanics of asynchronous execution, microtask queues, and Promise combinators (`Promise.all`, `Promise.race`, `Promise.any`) to keep the laboratory running.

### Features

- **Dynamic Steam Physics Engine:** Animated SVG pipeline elements where glowing steam travels through the connections based on code-defined resolve/reject timings.
- **Steampunk Boiler Dashboard:** A retro control dashboard allowing players to configure async wait times, resolve/reject values, and trigger delayed network simulations.
- **Advanced Flow Junctions:** Interactive puzzles showcasing the differences between Promise combinators by routing steam to multiple gauges simultaneously.
- **Sanity / Boiler Pressure Gauge:** Unhandled Promise rejections increase boiler pressure, triggering alarms and screen-shake effects when close to a meltdown.
- **Interactive Schematics Log:** A built-in field manual explaining synchronous vs asynchronous flow, macro/micro task prioritization in Promises, and correct error-handling patterns.

quests.name: Prototype Chain Séance
quests.shortDescription: A gothic mansion simulator that teaches JavaScript prototype inheritance by treating objects as rooms and prototypes as ghostly ancestors.
quests.longDescription:
## Prototype Chain Séance

Welcome to the haunted mansion of JavaScript. Here, objects are rooms, properties are furniture, and inheritance is a family legacy passed down through ghostly ancestors. To find the item you seek, you must trace the prototype chain—moving through doorways from descendant objects to their parents—until you reach the ancestor who possesses the property.

### Features

- **Ancestral Inheritance:** Missing properties are empty pedestals. You must navigate to the correct prototype room to find the inherited data.
- **Literal Shadowing:** A unique visual mechanic where properties in descendant objects mask (shadow) those in parent objects, making the ancestral version appear faded and inaccessible.
- **Object.create() Constructor:** Build new rooms and link them to parents, watching in real-time as inherited furniture ghosts into the descendant's space.
- **Gothic Mystery:** Solve a 4-generation chain puzzle to unlock the mansion's final secret, avoiding decoys that send you back to the cellar.
- **Atmospheric Visuals:** A premium, dark-themed UI featuring flickering lighting, gothic architectural SVG art, and high-contrast, moody color palettes.
- **Zero-Dependency Engine:** A flawlessly responsive, self-contained interactive lesson using only Vanilla JS and native CSS.

quests.name: Regex CSS Golf
quests.shortDescription: Master CSS selectors through interactive golf-style challenges by writing the shortest valid selector to match target elements.
quests.longDescription:
## Regex CSS Golf

Sharpen your CSS selector skills with golf-style puzzles. Write the shortest valid CSS selector that matches only the highlighted target elements while avoiding all others. Progress through increasingly challenging levels covering combinators, pseudo-classes, attribute selectors, and structural selectors.

### Features

- Progressive CSS selector challenges
- Real-time validation and feedback
- Golf scoring based on selector length
- Learn combinators, attribute selectors, and pseudo-classes
- Local progress saving using localStorage
- Fully offline, single-file quest

quests.name: Regex Golf
quests.shortDescription: Write the shortest regex to separate two sets of strings. 10 holes, par scoring, localStorage progress.
quests.longDescription:
## Regex Golf

A golf-themed regex puzzle game with 10 progressively harder holes.

### Features

- Real-time matching feedback as you type
- Par scoring system (eagle, birdie, par, bogey)
- 3-star rating per hole
- Progress saved via localStorage
- Full scorecard with totals
- Confetti on perfect scores

quests.name: Rewind: A Reflog Heist
quests.shortDescription: A spy-heist thriller that teaches git reflog — Git's most underused safety net for recovering lost commits.
quests.longDescription:
## Rewind: A Reflog Heist

Commits have gone missing — force-pushed over, reset away, deleted with their branch. Scan the reflog terminal, find the real lead among convincing decoys, and extract the lost work before blowing your cover.

### Features

- Spy-thriller theme with a neon red-string case board and HUD-style accents
- Real git reflog mechanics, including HEAD@{n} references and realistic decoy entries
- Risk meter creates tension without punishing hard failure — three wrong picks means a retry, not a dead end
- Five jobs of escalating difficulty, the final job requiring both the right entry and the right recovery command
- Field Manual explaining real reflog syntax and commands
- Job completion saved locally
- Fully responsive, keyboard accessible, respects reduced-motion preferences
- Works completely offline

quests.name: Selector Sniper
quests.shortDescription: An Old West shooting gallery game that trains you to rapidly identify DOM elements using CSS selectors.
quests.longDescription:
## Selector Sniper

Step up to the shooting gallery and test your CSS targeting reflexes. In this Old West arcade game, DOM elements pop up like wooden targets. You are given a CSS selector "bounty" (such as `.danger` or `ul > li:nth-child(2)`) and must fire at the correct HTML elements before they drop.

### Features

- **Arcade Action:** Targets pop up and down on spring timers. Hit the right target to spin a coin; miss, and the target "shoots back" with a screen-shake and a rolling tumbleweed.
- **Escalating Bounties:** Difficulty scales seamlessly from basic element tags and classes up to advanced combinators and pseudo-classes.
- **Wanted Poster Scorecard:** After the dust settles, receive a detailed breakdown of your speed and accuracy across different selector types to see exactly where your CSS targeting needs practice.
- **Premium SVG Aesthetics:** Highly detailed, procedurally drawn wooden signs, targets, and Old West environments built entirely natively.
- **Zero-Dependency Engine:** Flawless responsive layout and 60fps animations built entirely in a single HTML file with Vanilla JS and CSS.

quests.name: Specificity Wars
quests.shortDescription: A high-stakes casino battle where CSS selectors are poker hands. Construct selectors to beat the House and learn the rules of the cascade.
quests.longDescription:
## Specificity Wars

Welcome to the casino of cascading styles. In this high-stakes game, CSS selectors are poker hands battling for control of an element's style. Construct your hand using Element, Class, ID, and !important cards, and watch the real-time specificity calculation unfold as you go head-to-head against the House.

### Features

- Real-time drag-and-drop gameplay to construct CSS selector strings on a premium felt casino table
- Live specificity score calculation displayed as physical stacking casino chips (0,0,0,0 format)
- Four distinct card tiers (!important, ID, Class, Element) with detailed SVG iconography and 3D flip animations
- Detailed Showdown Breakdown engine that visually compares the specificity array tier-by-tier to explain exactly why a hand won or lost
- Dynamic, fully responsive scaling architecture built with CSS clamp() and viewport units to fit any screen perfectly
- Comprehensive Field Guide modal explaining the real-world application and dangers of each selector tier
- Works completely offline as a self-contained engine

quests.name: SQL Query Challenge
quests.shortDescription: Write queries to match expected output tables.
quests.longDescription:
## SQL Query Challenge

An interactive quest to test and improve your SQL skills. Write queries to transform input tables into the expected output.

### Features

- Write SQL queries in a browser-based editor.
- Compare your query output against the target table.
- Multiple levels of increasing difficulty.
- Progress saved locally.

quests.name: Strata: The Lost Commits
quests.shortDescription: A field excavation of version history; an interactive Git rebase simulator that turns commit history cleanup into an archaeological dig.
quests.longDescription:
## Strata: The Lost Commits

Four sites, each hiding a scattered commit history beneath the soil. You are an archivist tasked with cleaning up messy git history before it merges into the main branch. Every digging tool corresponds to a real `git rebase -i` command.

### Features

- Four distinct excavation sites, each with unique historical stratigraphy (commit history)
- Authentic Git mechanics mapped to archaeological tools: Pick (keep), Reword (rename), Squash (bond), Fixup (patch), and Drop (shatter)
- Preservation meter that challenges you to perform the most accurate rebase possible
- Interactive Finds Table to reorder and finalize your commit history
- In-game Archivist's Guide explaining the Git theory behind each dig
- Analog-archaeological aesthetic with hand-drawn SVG elements and custom fonts
- Progress saved locally via localStorage
- Fully self-contained single HTML file with no external dependencies

quests.name: Terminal Speed Run
quests.shortDescription: Complete CLI tasks against the clock in a retro terminal.
quests.longDescription:
## Terminal Speed Run

A quest to test your command-line speed and accuracy. Race against the timer to complete a series of CLI tasks in a simulated terminal.

### Features

- Retro terminal UI with scanlines
- Live timer to track your speed
- A series of challenges to test your CLI knowledge
- Progress and best times saved locally

quests.name: The Alchemist's Ledger
quests.shortDescription: A medieval apothecary simulator that teaches JavaScript variables, scope, and type coercion through interactive potion brewing.
quests.longDescription:
## The Alchemist's Ledger

Step into the apothecary and learn the fundamental rules of JavaScript variables through tactile chemistry. JavaScript data types, scope, and mutability are represented as potion ingredients and brewing apparatuses. Mixing incompatible types causes visible chemical reactions, teaching JS type coercion rules without reading a single console error.

### Features

- **Visual Type Coercion:** Drag ingredient vials (typed values) into a cauldron. String and number concatenation is shown as literal color-blending chemistry.
- **Tangible Immutability:** `const` vials are sealed with wax. Attempting to "re-pour" (reassign) one shatters the glass with animated particle effects, teaching immutability through direct consequence.
- **Nested Scope Chambers:** Understand lexical scope through physical constraints. Ingredients move through a Global Cauldron → Function Flask → Block Vial, and are only usable in chambers they were poured into.
- **Recipe Book Progression:** Complete JS logic challenges disguised as potion recipes to earn glowing bottled potions for your alchemy shelf.
- **Premium SVG Art:** High-fidelity, procedurally drawn laboratory equipment built natively with zero external dependencies.

quests.name: The Branch That Wouldn't Die
quests.shortDescription: A survival-horror game that teaches branch hygiene — merge, rebase, or delete runaway branches before the chaos meter fills.
quests.longDescription:
## The Branch That Wouldn't Die

Branches keep spawning and diverging on a live, growing tree. Some are finished features, some are drifting behind main, some are already dead experiments — but they all look alive. Click a branch and choose the right action before the chaos meter fills.

### Features

- Live SVG branch-tree visualization with real overlap-prevention logic, not a static diagram
- Direct manipulation gameplay — click any branch, choose merge, rebase, or delete
- Three real branch types (ready, drifting, stale) each requiring a genuinely different correct action
- Smoothly interpolated chaos meter with a real panic-shake past 80%, never a snap-jump
- Escalating spawn rate and required branch spacing as survival time increases
- Field Guide explaining when to merge, rebase, or delete, accessible anytime
- Best survival time saved locally
- Fully responsive, keyboard accessible, respects reduced-motion preferences
- Works completely offline

quests.name: The Event Loop
quests.shortDescription: A time-loop horror mystery that teaches the JavaScript event loop — schedule tasks across a live call stack, microtask and macrotask queues, and render pipeline to survive each 3:00 AM reset.
quests.longDescription:
## The Event Loop

2:59 AM. A production incident. At 3:00 AM sharp the office resets — the same minute, over and over. You are trapped in an event loop, literally, and a ghostly figure called The Blocking Call stalks the corridors freezing everything it touches. Escape by scheduling your actions on the right queues so they execute in the right order and survive each reset.

### Features

- Live visualized JavaScript runtime: call stack, microtask queue, macrotask queue, and render pipeline drawn as four glowing conveyor tracks with task cards animating between them in true spec order.
- Draggable task cards for `setTimeout(fn, 0)`, `Promise.resolve().then(fn)`, `queueMicrotask`, `requestAnimationFrame`, and synchronous calls — predicting execution order is the puzzle, not a quiz.
- Honest engine: the player's plan runs through real native browser scheduling and is observed live, so the ordering shown is the browser's actual behavior. The browser itself is the game engine.
- Four loops teaching distinct concepts: the call stack (recursive memories that crush you), micro vs macro ordering (two doors, two timings), microtask starvation (a possessed `.then` chain that stops the screen painting), and yielding to the renderer before a 10-second blocking loop lands.
- Loop-counter fail system with `console.warn`-flavored rewind text and glitch effects that intensify with each reset, plus a synthetic Web Audio clock-tick heartbeat that stutters when the main thread blocks — zero external audio files.
- Fully responsive single-file build in raw Vanilla JS and CSS variables with `prefers-reduced-motion` support and absolutely zero external frameworks.

quests.name: The Merge Conflict from Hell
quests.shortDescription: A horror-themed terminal escape room that teaches real Git commands — staging, detached HEAD, conflict resolution, and force pushing — under a draining sanity meter.
quests.longDescription:
## The Merge Conflict from Hell

Midnight. The server room door locks itself. The spirit of a corrupted Senior Developer — The Detached HEAD — has trapped your soul in a dirty working tree. Escape by typing real Git commands into a possessed Bash terminal across four corrupted branches, before your sanity hits zero and you are hard-deleted from `main`.

### Features

- Fully interactive faux Bash terminal that parses and evaluates real Git commands: `git add`, `commit`, `checkout -b`, `rebase`, and `push --force`, with honest `git status` output as the core teaching mechanic.
- Four escalating levels: selective staging in a haunted component tree, a maze where phantom commits detach HEAD and invert your controls, a three-hunk merge conflict resolver, and a 60-second rapid-fire command finale.
- 100% synthetic Web Audio horror soundscape — looping server-room hum, jumpscare screeches, and a heartbeat whose tempo tracks your remaining sanity. No audio files.
- Reactive dread systems: sanity meter, screen shake, CRT scanlines, canvas static bursts, random scheduled jumpscares, and whole-page micro-glitches that intensify as you fail.
- Digital blood splatter victory sequence via canvas-confetti, plus command history recall, per-level hints, and Git-flavoured easter eggs (`git blame`, `sudo`, `git reset --hard`).
- Graceful degradation with library fallbacks and full `prefers-reduced-motion` support that kills the theatrics but keeps the game playable.
